item.title: Firewall rulesMatch() ignores destination — rules to different IPs on same port match as equal
item.intent: Firewall verification must compare all rule fields including destination. Missing destination comparison means rules allowing traffic to unintended IPs go undetected.
item.description: Code review found that src/build/launcher/firewall.ts rulesMatch() only compares target, protocol, and dport — not the destination IP/domain. Two rules with different destinations but same port are considered equivalent, so verifyFirewall() may report correct state when rules are actually wrong.
steps: Add destination field to rulesMatch() comparison
Update FirewallRuleDescriptor type if destination is not already included
Add test cases for same-port-different-destination scenarios
acceptanceCriteria: rulesMatch() compares target, protocol, dport, AND destination
verifyFirewall() correctly detects when a rule exists with wrong destination
Existing tests updated to cover destination field comparison
